toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Remove legacy AssemblyInfo.cs and Properties dir

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a full picture. Let me analyze the legacy patterns and modernize to SDK-style format.

Legacy patterns identified:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and `xmlns` — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` imports** — SDK-style handles these automatically
3. **Explicit `Configuration`/`Platform` defaults** — SDK-style provides these by default
4. **`ProjectGuid`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` (or can upgrade to .NET 6/8+)
6. **`FileAlignment`**, **`AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`**, and explicit Debug/Release property groups — all defaults in SDK-style that can be removed
7. **Explicit framework `<Reference>` items** like System and System.Core — implicit in SDK-style for .NET Framework
8. **`<Compile>` items** — SDK-style automatically includes all `.cs` files through globbing
9. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style auto-generates this; assembly metadata moves directly into the csproj `<PropertyGroup>`
10. **`OutputType`, `RootNamespace`, and `AssemblyName`** — all defaults that can be omitted

The modernized file will be much leaner. I'll keep `net472` as the target since that's a separate decision from the project format migration, but I'll note the upgrade option. Now I'll create the simplified csproj and remove the AssemblyInfo file.

</Project>

data.content:
Here's a summary of every legacy pattern found and what was done:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `ToolsVersion` + `xmlns` on `<Project>` | → `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| 2 | Explicit `Microsoft.Common.props` import | Removed — SDK handles it |
| 3 | Explicit `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` import | Removed — SDK handles it |
| 4 | `Configuration`/`Platform` condition defaults | Removed — SDK defaults |
| 5 | `ProjectGuid` | Removed — not needed |
| 6 | `OutputType` Library | Removed — it's the default |
| 7 | `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` | Removed — inferred from filename |
| 8 | `TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2 | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 9 | `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | Removed — SDK defaults |
| 10 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | Removed — SDK provides sensible defaults |
| 11 | `<Reference>` for System.\* assemblies | Removed — implicit for .NET Framework |
| 12 | `<Compile Include="...">` items | Removed — SDK uses automatic file globbing |
| 13 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Deleted — metadata moved to `<PropertyGroup>` (auto-generated by SDK) |
| 14 | `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | Removed — default for SDK-style |

The project went from **47 lines → 11 lines**. If you later want to target modern .NET, just change `net472` to e.g. `net8.0`.
